page: Amazon Web Services r7iz.metal-32xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features Intel Xeon 6455B processors, delivering a total of 64 physical cores and 128 vCPUs with a dedicated allocation. Operating at a high base clock speed of 3.9 GHz, the processors are equipped with 60 MB of L3 cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache. The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations, alongside hardware-assisted cryptography and virtualization.

The instance is equipped with 1024.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per physical core. While the server does not include local NVMe storage, it features a high-speed network interface with a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps. This configuration is designed for workloads requiring massive memory capacity and high-throughput network connectivity without relying on local disk storage.

In benchmark testing, the r7iz.metal-32xl demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple categories. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench scores place the instance in the top 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks also rank in the top tier, reflecting the efficiency of the 5 GHz DDR5 memory. Database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput similarly achieve top-tier classifications. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal configuration eliminates virtualization overhead, maximizing the return on high-density compute and memory resources for enterprise workloads.

Given its massive memory footprint, high CPU clock speed, and advanced instruction sets, the r7iz.metal-32xl is highly suited for memory-intensive and compute-heavy enterprise applications. Key workloads include large-scale relational databases, in-memory data stores like Redis, high-performance computing simulations, electronic design automation, and intensive software compilation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.metal-32xl is a high-performance, memory-optimized bare-metal server featuring 128 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6455B processors running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory, offering 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage. Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, supported by advanced hardware instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. This bare-metal configuration is highly efficient for demanding workloads such as large-scale in-memory databases, electronic design automation, software compilation, and high-performance computing applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.metal-32xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server featuring 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier database and multi-core compute performance.

tagline: A high-frequency bare-metal server combining one terabyte of DDR5 memory with powerful multi-core processing for demanding enterprise database workloads.

1024.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage
Optimized for in-memory databases, EDA, and high-performance computing
